title: Deaths from terrorism, by target
subtitle: Confirmed deaths, including all victims and attackers who died as a result of the incident.
caption:
Deaths from terrorism, by target

Confirmed deaths, including all victims and attackers who died as a result of the incident.

This horizontal bar chart displays the number of confirmed deaths from terrorism worldwide in 2021, categorized by the specific target of the incident. The data is arranged in descending order, with the longest bars at the top representing the highest casualty counts. "Private citizens & property" constitutes the largest category, recording 4,142 deaths. The "Military" follows closely with 3,782 deaths, and "Police" accounts for 1,419 deaths. These three categories represent the highest values on the chart, significantly exceeding all others. "Government" targets resulted in 712 deaths, while "Militia" and "Business" targets account for 518 and 347 deaths respectively. A distinct drop in values occurs after the business category. "Transportation" shows 137 deaths, followed by "Educational institutions" with 126, "Diplomatic" targets with 106, and "Religious targets" with 102. The lower portion of the chart features categories with fewer than 40 deaths each. "Unknown" targets account for 37 deaths, "NGO" for 30, and "Journalists & media" for 22. Several categories register very low numbers, including "Other" (17), "Violent political parties" (16), "Telecommunication" (6), "Utilities" (4), "Airports and airplanes" (3), and "Maritime" (2). The chart concludes with "Tourists," "Food or water supply," and "Abortion related" targets, all of which show a value of 0 for the year 2021.
